audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for consecration of firstborn, unleavened bread memorial, teaching children, route by wilderness, Joseph's bones, pillar of cloud/fire.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Consecrate to me every firstborn, opening every womb among children of Israel, among human and among beast; it is mine.

Moses said to the people, Remember this day on which you went out from Egypt, from house of servants/slaves, for by strength of hand YHWH brought you out from this; and no leavened bread shall be eaten.
Today you are going out, in month of Aviv.

And it shall be when YHWH brings you into land of Canaanite, Hittite, Amorite, Hivite, and Jebusite, which he swore to your fathers to give you, a land flowing with milk and honey, that you shall serve this service in this month.
Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, and on seventh day festival to YHWH.
Unleavened bread shall be eaten seven days; no leavened bread shall be seen with you, and no leaven shall be seen with you in all your border.
You shall tell your child on that day, saying, It is because of what YHWH did for me when I came out from Egypt.
It shall be for you as sign upon your hand and as memorial between your eyes, so that Torah/instruction of YHWH may be in your mouth, for with strong hand YHWH brought you out from Egypt.
You shall keep this statute at its appointed time from days to days.
And it shall be when YHWH brings you into land of Canaanite, as he swore to you and your fathers, and gives it to you,
you shall make pass over to YHWH every opener of womb, and every opener of young among beasts that belongs to you; the males are YHWH's.
Every firstborn donkey you shall redeem with lamb; and if you do not redeem, you shall break its neck. Every firstborn of human among your sons you shall redeem.
And it shall be when your child asks you tomorrow, saying, What is this? you shall say to him, By strength of hand YHWH brought us out from Egypt, from house of servants/slaves.

And it happened, when Pharaoh was hard against sending us, YHWH killed every firstborn in land of Egypt, from firstborn of human to firstborn of beast; therefore I sacrifice to YHWH every opener of womb, the males, and every firstborn of my sons I redeem.
It shall be as sign upon your hand and as bands between your eyes, for by strength of hand YHWH brought us out from Egypt.
When Pharaoh sent the people, Divine did not lead them by way of land of Philistines, though it was near, for Divine said, Lest the people change mind when they see war and return to Egypt.
So Divine turned the people by way of wilderness toward Sea of Reeds; and children of Israel went up armed/arrayed from land of Egypt.
Moses took bones of Joseph with him, for he had made children of Israel surely swear, saying, Visiting, Divine will visit you, and you shall bring up my bones from this with you.
They journeyed from Succoth and camped at Etham, at edge of wilderness.
YHWH went before them by day in pillar of cloud to guide them by the way, and by night in pillar of fire to give them light, so they could go by day and by night.
The pillar of cloud by day and pillar of fire by night did not turn aside from before the people.

translation_notes: Firstborn consecration responds to firstborn deliverance/judgment with redemption, not death as ongoing practice.
Unleavened bread is pedagogy: children ask, parents narrate liberation.
Torah/instruction in mouth, hand, and between eyes roots command in embodied memory.
Sea of Reeds is used rather than Red Sea; route avoids immediate war as trauma-aware Divine guidance.
safety_note: Named land peoples require later ethical audit. Firstborn language should emphasize redemption of human firstborn and memory of liberation, not ongoing violence.
